text: These are the generations of Esau, that is Edom.
Esau took his wives from the Canaanite women: Adah, Oholibamah, and also Basemath.
Basemath was Ishmael's daughter, sister of Nebaioth.
Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau; Basemath bore Reuel.
Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah; these were Esau's sons born in Canaan.
Esau took his wives, sons, daughters, household, livestock, and property gathered in Canaan, and moved away from Jacob.
Their combined possessions were too great for the land to support them both together.
So Esau settled in the hill country of Seir; Esau is Edom.
These are the generations of Esau, father of the Edomites, in the hill country of Seir.
These are Esau's sons' names: Eliphaz, son of Adah, and Reuel, son of Basemath.
Eliphaz's sons were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
Timna, a concubine of Eliphaz, bore Amalek to him.
Reuel's sons were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
Oholibamah's sons by Esau were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
These were the chiefs among Esau's sons: the sons of Eliphaz, Esau's firstborn.
The chiefs listed descended from Eliphaz in the land of Edom, sons of Adah.
These were the chiefs descended from Reuel, Esau's son, sons of Basemath.
These were the chiefs descended from Oholibamah, Esau's wife.
These were Esau's sons, and these were their chiefs; Esau is Edom.
These were the sons of Seir the Horite, inhabitants of the land.
These were the chiefs of the Horites, sons of Seir, in the land of Edom.
Lotan's sons were Hori and Hemam; Lotan's sister was Timna.
Shobal's sons were Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah, who found hot springs in the wilderness while tending his father's donkeys.
Anah's children were Dishon and Oholibamah.
Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
These were the chiefs of the Horites: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, chiefs of the Horites according to their clans in Seir.
These were the kings who reigned in Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites.
Bela son of Beor reigned in Edom, his city named Dinhabah.
When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.
When Jobab died, Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in Moab's territory, reigned, his city named Avith.
When Hadad died, Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.
When Samlah died, Shaul of Rehoboth-on-the-river reigned in his place.
When Shaul died, Baal-hanan son of Achbor reigned in his place.
When Baal-hanan died, Hadar reigned, his city named Pau, and his wife was Mehetabel, daughter of Matred.
These were the chiefs descended from Esau, by their names, according to their clans and territories.
Chief Timna, Chief Alvah, Chief Jetheth.
Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon.
Chief Magdiel and Chief Iram; these were Edom's chiefs according to their settlements in the land they possessed; Esau was the father of the Edomites.
